title: Complete the PyO3-to-IPC cutover and delete the PyO3 transport
description: THE keystone. Both transports are fully built and the App is transport-agnostic (Box<dyn BridgeService>), but Cargo.toml:15 still ships default=["pyo3-bridge"], so a flagless cargo build runs the deprecated in-process PyO3 path (src/main.rs:360-368). Flip the default to IPC, delete the pyo3-gated code in src/bridge.rs (init_python_backend, bridge_worker_loop, dispatch_rpc, BridgeHandle, ~26 PyO3 sites) and configure_python_env in main.rs, remove the optional pyo3 dep (Cargo.toml:32), then unify the two Python JSON-RPC dispatch registries (server/_handlers.py HANDLER_REGISTRY vs api.py CrystalController._rpc_registry) into one domain.verb table and fix the init-error-masked-as-Method-not-found bug. Realizes ADR-014; finishes ADR-006's keystone.
design:

title: [remove-hardcoded-ssh-password] Remove committed root/adminadmin SSH credentials from cluster config
description:
python/crystalmath/high_level/clusters.py hardcodes ssh_user="root", ssh_password="adminadmin" for vasp-01/02/03 (lines 257-258, 275-276, 293-294) in the module-level BEEFCAKE2_NODES dict. The value is actively used: interpolated into `sshpass -p '...'` (line 154, exposing it in the process table) and passed to AiiDA computer configuration with look_for_keys disabled (lines 1468-1470). docs/workflows/cluster-setup.md reproduces the password as the canonical credential. title: [fix-pytest-collection-aiosqlite] Unbreak pytest collection (aiosqlite import in deprecated tui/ aborts the whole suite)
description:

AREA: Test suite / CI health
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: True
KEY FILES: pyproject.toml, tui/src/core/materials_api/cache.py, tui/src/core/materials_api/__init__.py, tui/pyproject.toml, scripts/pre-push-quality.sh

title: Remove all simulated/mock success paths
description: Eliminate the {'simulated':True} no-runner branch, dry-run placeholder result, and silent SLURM->simulation degradation in runners.py so no fabricated result crosses IPC.
design:
## Deepening design — C2-analysis-runner-seam
**Risk:** high · **Depends on IPC cutover:** False

### Current shape (shallowness / friction)
runners.py is 2,488 lines. BaseAnalysisRunner(ABC) at line 208 is a deep-but-leaky god-class: a small public Interface (run() line 875, run_async() line 969, read-only properties) hides a large Implementation but bakes three execution policies into the production path instead of behind a seam. The runner field self._runner (type WorkflowRunner or None) is set in __init__ lines 290-297: explicit runner, then auto SLURM via _create_slurm_runner, then None. WorkflowRunner is already a real Protocol (protocols.py line 304, submit() at line 330). So a seam adapter slot exists but only ONE real adapter is wired (SLURMWorkflowRunner); the second is a None-check stub, not a type. Three baked-in branches: 1) no-runner stub in _execute_step lines 1187-1208: if self._runner is None, return StepResult(success=True, outputs simulated True) when metadata allow_stub_execution is True, else raise NoRunnerConfiguredError. 2) dry-run stub _create_dry_run_result lines 1371-1388, reached only from run() line 943 when config.dry_run. 3) real path lines 1210-1227 wraps self._runner.submit(). Pure builders reachable today ONLY through run()/_build_workflow_steps(): _load_structure 471, _select_code 708, _get_parameters 748, which calls _get_protocol_parameters 776 and _get_code_parameters 814. Verified at runtime these are pure of self._runner, IPC, and PyO3 (an instance with _runner None returns correct params).

### Deepened interface
Make execution policy a real two-adapter seam and make each builder a first-class testable Interface, so the god-class is Deep (small interface, behaviour behind adapters) not leaky. Part 1, execution adapters using the existing WorkflowRunner Protocol (protocols.py line 304): require a WorkflowRunner at construction, remove the None branch, and add a concrete TestDoubleRunner implementing WorkflowRunner whose submit() returns a deterministic WorkflowResult (the moral equivalent of the simulated stub and of _create_dry_run_result). That second adapter turns a hypothetical seam into a real one; dry-run becomes inject TestDoubleRunner rather than a special if config.dry_run branch plus a bespoke _create_dry_run_result. _execute_step collapses to validate dependencies, call self._runner.submit(), wrap result: no None-check, no allow_stub_execution, no NoRunnerConfiguredError. Locality: simulation behaviour lives in ONE class, not smeared across _execute_step and _create_dry_run_result. Part 2, builder interfaces: promote the pure builders to a public side-effect-free surface (free functions or a small WorkflowPlanner collaborator): select_code(), parameters_for(workflow_type, code, protocol, overrides), load_structure(source). The interface is the test surface: each tested at its own boundary instead of through run() with patched _select_code. Leverage: callers inject a runner (real SLURM or TestDouble) and unit-test planners; the six subclasses keep only _build_workflow_steps and _get_default_resources.

### Seam & adapters
Seam: the WorkflowRunner Protocol (protocols.py line 304, contract submit() at line 330 returning WorkflowResult). It already exists, so this is the natural seam; _execute_step already programs to it on the real path (lines 1212-1218). Adapter 1 (real, exists): SLURMWorkflowRunner in crystalmath/integrations/slurm_runner.py, instantiated by _create_slurm_runner line 363 when cluster.scheduler is slurm. Adapter 2 (to add): TestDoubleRunner implementing WorkflowRunner returning a deterministic WorkflowResult. Today there is NO second adapter, only a None value with two in-method behaviours (stub-success vs raise) plus a separate dry-run builder, which is a hypothetical seam (one adapter). Adding TestDoubleRunner makes it a real seam (two adapters) and lets us delete the None branch, _create_dry_run_result, and NoRunnerConfiguredError. Deletion test: delete BaseAnalysisRunner and complexity reappears across the six subclasses (StandardAnalysis 1441, OpticalAnalysis 1609, PhononAnalysis 1798, ElasticAnalysis 1948, TransportAnalysis 2073, NonlinearOpticsAnalysis 2198), so it earns its keep as orchestration. But the None-stub branch fails the deletion test the other way: delete it and complexity vanishes (it only protects callers who forgot to inject a runner, a job for the type system or a TestDouble, not an if-branch). That branch is shallow and should be deleted once adapter 2 exists.

### Migration steps
Full change is NOT safe-now because it rewrites the seven stub-guard tests. Steps: 1) Add TestDoubleRunner implementing WorkflowRunner (name test-double, is_available True, submit returns a deterministic WorkflowResult success True). 2) Replace config.dry_run handling: run() lines 941-945 injects TestDoubleRunner instead of calling _create_dry_run_result; delete _create_dry_run_result lines 1371-1388. 3) Make runner required: in __init__ lines 290-297 raise if no runner can be resolved, or default to TestDoubleRunner only when an explicit dry_run/test flag is set; delete the self._runner None path. 4) Simplify _execute_step lines 1187-1208: delete the None-check, allow_stub_execution, the simulated return, and NoRunnerConfiguredError plus its export at line 2473. 5) Rewrite TestExecuteStepStubGuard (test_high_level_api.py 933-1018) into a TestDoubleRunner-injection suite. 6) Extract _select_code/_get_parameters/_load_structure into a public WorkflowPlanner with direct unit tests; update the three patch.object _select_code sites in test_workflows.py (247, 268, 447) to call the public planner. Sequenced under epic delete-phase3-aspiration / ADR-019. Independent of the PyO3 to IPC cutover (these builders and policies are pure-Python; the WorkflowRunner seam is unchanged by transport). Safe-now subset: promote ONE pure builder to a public alias method, see obviousFix.

### Tests (survive / add)
Tests that must be REWRITTEN by the full design (do NOT survive deletion of the stub path): TestExecuteStepStubGuard in python/tests/test_high_level_api.py lines 933-1018, which is 7 tests (the prompt's ~10 is an overcount; verified 7 collected and passed). They assert exactly the contract being deleted: outputs equals simulated True (line 991) and NoRunnerConfiguredError raised (942, 952, 962, 972, 994, 1010) plus the truthy-vs-True opt-in nuance (972). Removing the None-branch makes all 7 obsolete; replace with tests that inject TestDoubleRunner and assert a missing runner is a construction-time error. Tests that SURVIVE or migrate cheaply: test_workflows.py _build_workflow_steps and _select_code cases (231, 252, 247/268/447) keep working; after extraction they call the public planner directly instead of patch.object _select_code. Tests to ADD: unit tests for parameters_for/select_code/load_structure at their own interface, plus a TestDoubleRunner.submit() determinism test. Safe-now ADD: one optional test asserting the new public get_parameters returns the same dict as the private _get_parameters (no behaviour change).

### Obvious-fix verdict
safe=True scope=python-only
Maximal safe-now slice: bounded (one added method), python-only (no Rust/Cargo/PyO3), cutover-independent (pure parameter math, no transport), verifiable by a one-line import-equality check plus the two guarding test files without a full suite run, and zero behaviour change (delegates, removes nothing). The FULL candidate (require WorkflowRunner, add TestDoubleRunner, delete the None-stub plus _create_dry_run_result plus NoRunnerConfiguredError) is NOT safe-now: it deletes the contract that the 7 TestExecuteStepStubGuard tests assert on, so those must be rewritten first. That belongs to epic delete-phase3-aspiration / ADR-019, design-only.
notes: Down payment applied on branch refactor/arch-deepening: added a public pure get_parameters() delegator on BaseAnalysisRunner (the first extracted builder). Full deepening (require WorkflowRunner adapter + TestDoubleRunner; extract _load_structure/_select_code/_build_workflow_steps; delete the {'simulated':True} no-runner path) remains.

title: Add build.rs codegen for src/models.rs

design:
## Deepening design — C5-wire-contract-codegen
**Risk:** high · **Depends on IPC cutover:** True

### Current shape (shallowness / friction)
src/models.rs (2,271 lines) is a fully hand-written serde mirror of the Python wire contract. Its own doc header admits it (src/models.rs:1-4: "These Rust structs match the Pydantic models in `python/crystalmath/models.py`"). It contains 45 structs/enums (grep `^(pub )?(struct|enum)`), 139 `#[serde(...)]` attributes, and 110 in-file `#[test]` functions. There is NO `include!`, NO build.rs, and NO `build=` key in Cargo.toml — it is 100% hand-authored.

The seam is the IPC boundary: src/ipc/client.rs returns `serde_json::Value` (client.rs:550-556, 616, 623), and 14 caller modules deserialize that Value into `models::*` types (src/app.rs, src/app_tests.rs, src/bridge.rs, src/state/mod.rs, and src/ui/{cluster_manager,jobs,mod,new_job,recipes,slurm_queue,vasp_input,workflow_config,workflow_dashboard,workflows}.rs) — 95 `models::` references total. Deletion test: complexity REAPPEARS across 14 callers, so models.rs earns its keep; this is a real seam (>=2 adapters), not a hypothetical one.

The drift surface is asymmetric and worse than the ADR states. The Python contract source is fragmented into three tiers: (1) 9 pydantic models in python/crystalmath/models.py (JobState, DftCode, RunnerType, SchedulerOptions, JobSubmission, JobStatus, JobDetails, ClusterConfig, StructureData) plus WorkflowType in python/crystalmath/protocols.py:76; (2) dataclasses in python/crystalmath/_vendor/core/materials_api/models.py (MaterialRecord, StructureResult — the de-facto source for MaterialResult/StructurePreview); (3) the overwhelming majority — ad-hoc `dict[str, Any]` shapes assembled inline in api.py / server/handlers (e.g. get_slurm_queue_json at api.py:1100-1124 returns `list[dict[str, Any]]` whose keys are documented only in a docstring; only jobs.py:67 uses `.model_dump()`). I verified this by name: of the 45 Rust types, exactly 9 have a same-named pydantic class anywhere outside _vendor; the other 36 (Template, SlurmQueueEntry, ClusterType, ClusterStatus, VaspInputFiles, GeneratedVaspInputs, LatticeParams, SymmetryInfo, StructurePreview, ValidationMessage/Result, MaterialResult/Properties, D12GenerationConfig, VaspPreset, VaspGenerationConfig, ApiResponse, ApiError, WorkflowAvailability/Status, Recipe, RecipesListResponse, WorkflowEngineStatus, the entire Quacc* family, ErrorSeverity, ErrorSuggestion, ParameterDefinition, ClusterConnectionResult, SlurmCancelResult) have NO pydantic source at all. The parity invariant is enforced only by AGENTS.md prose (ADR-016 calls this requirement D3) and by hand. A rename on the Python side breaks a TUI tab at runtime, not `cargo build` — exactly the failure ADR-016 describes.

### Deepened interface
Make python/crystalmath/models.py (pydantic) the single source of truth for the wire contract and reduce the Rust caller's interface to ONE thing it must know: "deserialize the boundary JSON into a generated type." The deep module is the pydantic model layer; the small interface is a single committed JSON Schema artifact (schema/wire-contract.json) plus a generated, never-hand-edited src/models_generated.rs. Leverage: today a field add edits two files (models.py + models.rs) plus risks 14 Rust callers silently; after, a field add edits one pydantic model and the Rust side either recompiles correctly or fails `cargo build`. Locality: the shape of every boundary payload lives in exactly one place (pydantic), and drift bugs collapse from "runtime, in whichever tab touches the field" to "compile time, at the generation step." The interface IS the test surface: the JSON Schema document is the assertable contract, and one round-trip test (ADR-016 §4) is the canary that the generator is wired correctly.

Tool choice: typify (oxidecomputer; crate `typify` / `cargo-typify`) compiles JSON Schema -> idiomatic Rust serde types, used in build.rs. This is already the ADR-016 decision (§2) and is the correct call over the three tools the candidate brief floated: (a) typeshare (1Password) INVERTS authority — it generates from annotated Rust serde types, making Rust the source of truth, the wrong direction for a project whose business logic/validation/result schemas all live in Python (ADR-006 decision 5); keep it only as a documented escape hatch for any future Rust-originated type (none exist today). (b) schemars derives JSON Schema FROM Rust types — also backwards, same problem as typeshare for the schema direction. (c) datamodel-code-generator (and any hand-maintained shared OpenAPI/JSON-Schema doc) introduces a THIRD artifact that can itself drift, when pydantic already emits JSON Schema for free via `model_json_schema()` (verified working on pydantic 2.12.5: `JobStatus.model_json_schema()` returns the expected property set). Net: pydantic emits the schema (no extra input artifact), typify consumes it (Python -> Rust flow), authority stays in Python.

### Seam & adapters
The seam is the JSON-RPC payload boundary at src/ipc/client.rs (`request_rpc(method, serde_json::Value) -> serde_json::Value`), where Value crosses from Python (serialized by api.py/server handlers) to Rust (deserialized into models::* by 14 caller modules). This is a REAL seam: two-plus independent adapters consume it (the production TUI callers in src/ui/*, and the test adapters in src/app_tests.rs and tests fixtures), so it earns a generated contract rather than a hand-mirror.

After deepening, the adapters become: (1) Python-side adapter — a new `crystalmath.contract` module with `export-schema [--check]` that collects every boundary-crossing pydantic model into one root model and serializes via `model_json_schema(ref_template=...)` to schema/wire-contract.json (the single committed artifact); (2) Rust-side adapter — build.rs runs typify over schema/wire-contract.json into `$OUT_DIR/wire_contract.rs`, which src/models.rs pulls in via `include!`, replacing the 36 hand-written boundary structs while retaining only Rust-only UI-state types (e.g. the `Color` mappings on JobState — those are UI concerns that must stay hand-written in an `impl` block on the generated enum). The contract version (a hash of the schema) is exchanged at the IPC handshake (ADR-016 §5) so an old binary vs new server fails fast with a typed error instead of a deep deserialize panic.

### Migration steps
Sequencing is gated by the IPC cutover (epic crystalmath-oho / ipc-cutover-delete-pyo3, NOT done — Cargo.toml line 14 still has `default = ["pyo3-bridge"]`), because the contract should protect the LIVE wire. Order:

1. (PREREQ, separate epic) Land the PyO3->IPC default flip so the JSON-RPC boundary is the real transport (crystalmath-oho.1).

2. WRITE THE MISSING PYDANTIC MODELS FIRST — the load-bearing step the ADR underplays. 36 of 45 Rust types have no pydantic source; the generator cannot emit them until they exist. Convert the `dict[str, Any]` returns in api.py / server/handlers (e.g. get_slurm_queue_json's SlurmQueueEntry, the Vasp*/D12 config shapes, the Quacc* family, Recipe/Workflow*, Material*) into pydantic BaseModels in models.py. This is the bulk of the effort and touches handler return statements (behavior-adjacent: `return dict` -> `return Model(...).model_dump(mode="json")`). Best landed incrementally, one method family at a time, each guarded by the existing test_models.py-style serialization tests. This also synergizes with ADR-009 (epic crystalmath-h67) which replaces the `key_results: Dict[str,Any]` blob with typed TaskDocuments — do the contract work as h67 lands fields, not before, to avoid double work.

3. Add `crystalmath.contract export-schema [--check]`; commit schema/wire-contract.json (no schema/ dir exists today). Wire `--check` into the Python CI lane (schema-freshness gate).

4. Add build.rs + typify dep; generate into `$OUT_DIR/wire_contract.rs`; `include!` from src/models.rs; delete the now-redundant hand-written boundary structs; move Rust-only UI helpers (JobState::color/as_str, etc.) into impl blocks on generated types. THIS IS THE RUST/BUILD-SYSTEM STEP — fragile (needs PYO3_PYTHON/build-tui.sh while PyO3 is live; the build.rs codegen must coexist with the feature-gated bridge), so it is design-only and must not be auto-applied.

5. Add the round-trip parity test (ADR-016 §4) extending tests/ipc_bridge_integration.rs and the CI codegen-freshness gate (ci-rust.yml). Add the contract-version handshake (§5) to client.rs/server.

This maps directly onto existing beads: epic crystalmath-ycz (children ycz.1 export-schema, ycz.2 build.rs codegen, ycz.3 CI drift check, ycz.4 AGENTS.md doc), depends-on crystalmath-oho, blocks crystalmath-h67 and crystalmath-7fs. ADR-016 §Migration impact is the authoritative checklist.

### Tests (survive / add)
Survive: the 110 in-file Rust tests in src/models.rs assert serde round-trips and field shapes — most should be regenerated-against or ported to assert against the generated types (the field-shape ones become redundant once typify guarantees the shape; the UI-helper tests like JobState::color stay). The 35 collectable tests in python/tests/test_models.py (verified `pytest --collect-only` -> 35 tests, including test_json_serialization_for_rust at line 205 which already pins exact field names for Rust) survive as-is and become the seed for the contract: each new pydantic model added in step 2 should carry an analogous model_dump_json field-name test.

Add: (1) a single round-trip integration test (ADR-016 §4) in tests/ipc_bridge_integration.rs asserting a representative payload per boundary type produced by the Python server deserializes into the generated Rust type and round-trips — the canary on the generation pipeline; (2) the schema-freshness CI gate (`export-schema --check`) and codegen-freshness gate (build.rs rebuild diff); (3) the demonstrative CI test that a pydantic field rename breaks `cargo build` (ADR-016 acceptance criterion 3). The interface is the test surface: schema/wire-contract.json becomes the assertable contract artifact.

### Obvious-fix verdict
safe=False scope=cross
Marked safe=false because the change fails every gate for an obvious fix: it is NOT bounded (rewrites a 2,271-line file feeding 14 callers and requires writing 36 new pydantic models), NOT Python-only (adds build.rs + typify, edits Cargo.toml, regenerates Rust), NOT cutover-independent (must protect the live IPC wire; coexists with the PyO3 feature gate), and NOT low-risk (deleting hand-tuned serde structs with 139 serde attributes risks behavior change across the TUI). The verificationCmd only confirms the PREREQUISITE (pydantic model_json_schema works on the 9 existing models and the Python test surface still collects) — it does not validate the candidate, underscoring that no NOW change exists.

title: Unify the two JSON-RPC dispatch registries into one domain.verb table
description: Collapse server/_handlers.py HANDLER_REGISTRY and api.py CrystalController._rpc_registry into a single dispatch surface.
design:
## Deepening design — C4-unify-dispatch-registries
**Risk:** high · **Depends on IPC cutover:** True

### Current shape (shallowness / friction)
Two JSON-RPC method tables exist, and which one answers a request depends entirely on the transport:

(1) `CrystalController._rpc_registry` — built in `python/crystalmath/api.py:178` (`_build_rpc_registry`), ~52 entries (api.py:189-273), keyed by a MIX of snake_case legacy names (`fetch_jobs`, `submit_job`, `cancel_job`, `create_cluster`, ...) and dotted `domain.verb` names (`recipes.list`, `clusters.list`, `jobs.list`, `vasp.generate_inputs`, `structures.preview`, ...). Consumed by `CrystalController.dispatch()` (api.py:275-389), which whitelists method_name against `_rpc_registry` (api.py:329) and returns -32601 otherwise.

(2) `HANDLER_REGISTRY` — defined in `python/crystalmath/server/_handlers.py:25`, populated by `@register_handler` decorators. It holds the 3 `system.*` lifecycle handlers in `_handlers.py:49,65,82` PLUS handlers auto-imported via `server/handlers/__init__.py:22-24` from `clusters.py` (`clusters.list`), `recipes.py` (`recipes.list`), and `jobs.py` (`jobs.list`, `jobs.submit`, `jobs.status`, `jobs.cancel`, `jobs.analyze_errors`, `jobs.get_output_file`). Consumed by `JsonRpcServer._dispatch` (server/__init__.py:213-287): it checks `HANDLER_REGISTRY` FIRST (line 258), then falls through to `controller.dispatch()` (line 269) for everything else.

CRITICAL: the two tables are NOT one redundant pair — they belong to two different transports.
- LIVE transport is PyO3 (`Cargo.toml:15` default=["pyo3-bridge"]). `src/bridge.rs:1159` calls `controller.dispatch()` DIRECTLY. grep confirms the PyO3 path never imports `server`/`HANDLER_REGISTRY`/`_dispatch`. So today every Rust request resolves through `_rpc_registry` ONLY; `server/__init__.py:_dispatch` and HANDLER_REGISTRY are dark code on the live path.
- FUTURE transport is the spawned-child stdio server whose entry is `JsonRpcServer._dispatch`, which layers HANDLER_REGISTRY in front of `controller.dispatch()`.

Observable consequences of the split, verified by grep on src/app.rs:
- `jobs.submit` (app.rs:3795), `jobs.status` (app.rs:2396), `jobs.get_output_file` (app.rs:5209) are sent by the live Rust app via `send_rpc`->dispatch_rpc->`controller.dispatch()`, but they exist ONLY in HANDLER_REGISTRY, NOT in `_rpc_registry` (grep of `"jobs.` in api.py returns only `jobs.list`). On the live PyO3 path these return -32601; they only work after the IPC cutover. These are quacc-path features that are effectively dead until cutover.
- `recipes.list`/`clusters.list`/`jobs.list` exist in BOTH tables. In the future IPC path HANDLER_REGISTRY shadows `_rpc_registry` (line 258 precedence), but on the LIVE PyO3 path only the `_rpc_registry` versions answer (the Rust app sends `recipes.list` at app.rs:4491 via send_rpc). The HANDLER_REGISTRY versions of these three mostly delegate back to the same controller methods (clusters.py:34 -> get_quacc_clusters_list; recipes.py:31 -> get_recipes_list) with an added controller-None fallback; `jobs.list` diverges slightly (handlers/jobs.py:55 builds JobStore directly; api.py:560 get_quacc_jobs_list wraps the same JobStore in try/except).

### Deepened interface
One Module — a single dispatch surface — with one Interface: `dispatch(method: str, params: dict) -> result | JsonRpcError`, keyed by ONE `domain.verb` namespace (jobs.list, jobs.submit, clusters.list, recipes.list, slurm.sync, vasp.generate_inputs, materials.search, ...). Callers (the single Rust `request_rpc`) need to know only: the verb name, its params, and that init failures surface as honest typed errors (ADR-003 taxonomy -32000/-32001/-32002/-32003), never -32601. -32601 means exactly "no such verb."

This is Deep: behind that one small string-keyed interface sits all backend selection (aiida vs sqlite), quacc JobStore access, SLURM sync, VASP generation, AI assistant, lifecycle. Today that depth is split behind TWO interfaces with TWO naming schemes and transport-dependent precedence, so a caller must know which transport is live to predict routing — the interface leaks the transport. Collapsing to one table makes "the interface is the test surface": one registry table can be enumerated and asserted in a single test, and the Rust namespace maps 1:1 (ADR-014 line 101).

### Seam & adapters
The Seam is the dispatch entry: `JsonRpcServer._dispatch` (server/__init__.py:213) is where the bifurcation lives (HANDLER_REGISTRY-first, then controller.dispatch fallthrough). There are TWO real adapters onto the registries, which proves the seam is real (one adapter would be hypothetical):
  Adapter A: `src/bridge.rs:dispatch_rpc` (PyO3) -> `controller.dispatch()` -> `_rpc_registry`.
  Adapter B: `src/ipc/client.rs` / future stdio server -> `JsonRpcServer._dispatch` -> HANDLER_REGISTRY then `_rpc_registry`.
Because two live adapters reach the tables through two different paths with different precedence, the seam earns its keep — but it is currently DUPLICATED, not unified. Deletion test: delete `_rpc_registry` and complexity REAPPEARS immediately (the live PyO3 transport loses every method it answers today, 16 snake_case verbs each with 1 Rust caller per grep, plus recipes/clusters/jobs.list). Delete HANDLER_REGISTRY and the future IPC transport loses system.* lifecycle and the quacc jobs.* verbs the Rust app already sends. Neither table is shallow; both earn their keep — which is exactly why this is a real consolidation and not a deletion.

### Migration steps
This IS the cutover work, sequenced behind it. Concretely (per ADR-014 section 3, lines 96-106):
1. (Cutover prerequisite) Flip default transport to IPC / spawned-child stdio (epic bead crystalmath-bc6 in ADR-014 line 221), so `JsonRpcServer._dispatch` becomes THE entry. Until this lands, removing anything from `_rpc_registry` breaks the live PyO3 path.
2. Define one canonical `domain.verb` table. Migrate every `_rpc_registry` snake_case entry to its dotted name (fetch_jobs->jobs.list, submit_job->jobs.submit, cancel_job->jobs.cancel, fetch_job_details->jobs.get, fetch_job_log->jobs.log, fetch_clusters->clusters.list, create_cluster->clusters.create, etc.). Drop the snake_case aliases (ADR-014 line 100).
3. Update the single Rust caller per verb in src/app.rs to send the dotted name (the cross-cutting part; ~16 call sites, each grep-confirmed as 1 src file).
4. Collapse HANDLER_REGISTRY and `_rpc_registry` into that one table; resolve the three currently-duplicated verbs (jobs.list/clusters.list/recipes.list) to a single implementation each, preferring the HANDLER_REGISTRY versions' controller-None fallback semantics.
5. Convert -32601-masked init failures (api.py masks controller=None as method-not-found) into honest ADR-003 typed errors (ADR-014 lines 103-106).
6. Verify with python/tests/test_jsonrpc_dispatch.py (extended to assert the table is enumerable and that no snake_case alias remains) plus tests/ipc_integration.rs on the Rust side.

### Tests (survive / add)
Surviving: python/tests/test_jsonrpc_dispatch.py (tests controller.dispatch for fetch_jobs, check_workflows_available, list_templates, the -32601 path at test_registry_security, positional params, null-id). These pin the CURRENT snake_case `_rpc_registry` behaviour and will need updating when aliases are dropped — they are the regression guard for routing. python/tests/test_handlers_quacc.py and python/tests/test_job_submission.py exercise HANDLER_REGISTRY (jobs.* handlers). Add: a single enumeration test asserting the unified table has exactly the expected `domain.verb` keys and zero snake_case keys (the interface-as-test-surface), and a parity test asserting every method the Rust app sends (grep-extracted from src/app.rs) is present in the one table. This converts "which transport answers this verb" from tribal knowledge into one assertable list (Locality: routing bugs and routing knowledge land in one place).

### Obvious-fix verdict
safe=False scope=cross
RPC routing breakage is high-impact and the constraint says be conservative. The candidate's premise (that _rpc_registry's three dotted entries are dead because HANDLER_REGISTRY shadows them) is FALSE under the live PyO3 transport — shadowing only applies in the not-yet-live stdio _dispatch. Deleting them breaks recipes.list/clusters.list/jobs.list on the running TUI. Any genuine unification must drop snake_case aliases and rename Rust call sites in lockstep, which is cross-language and gated on the PyO3->IPC cutover (Cargo.toml still default pyo3-bridge). This is design-only, owned by bead crystalmath-oho.4 under epic crystalmath-oho.

Build typed make_*_flow factories (relax/scf/bands) with thin Makers for CRYSTAL23/YAMBO, real multi-step execution (dependency chaining + wait-for-completion + inter-step data handoff — this is bd-0gs, currently patched on the doomed bespoke runner), and ADR-013 typed CodeHandoff edges over a closed HandoffArtifact enum with MANDATORY pre-submission restart-file validation (file match + checksum + source-completion + parallelization consistency). title: Generate Rust serde types from pydantic models to kill wire drift
description: Two hand-synced copies of the wire contract exist: src/models.rs (~30 structs/enums, header literally says 'These Rust structs match the Pydantic models in models.py') and python/crystalmath/models.py. Every field add edits both by hand; a rename breaks a TUI tab at runtime, not cargo build at CI. There is no build.rs, no typeshare/schemars/datamodel-codegen, no JSON Schema export. Make pydantic the source of truth: export JSON Schema from models.py and GENERATE src/models.rs in build.rs so drift becomes a compile error. Per the completeness critique this is drift-PREVENTION and must land EARLY (right after the IPC cutover makes the wire real), BEFORE the data-model churn of ADR-009 — otherwise every TaskDocument field gets hand-mirrored. Realizes ADR-016.

title: [aiida-launcher-import-fix] Fix AiiDA launcher imports broken by sys.path insertion / wrong namespace
description:
launch_geometry_optimization and launch_band_structure in python/crystalmath/workflows/aiida_launcher.py always fail. The module inserts tui/src at sys.path[0] (lines 27-30), which makes `from aiida import orm` (line 174) resolve to the local tui/src/aiida package that does not export orm — so the try block fails at line 174 before reaching the workchain import at 176/271. If aiida-core is pre-imported elsewhere the failure flips to the workchain line instead. Either way both functions return success=False. Verification confirmed P1 but corrected the mechanism (sys.path shadowing, not just a wrong workchain namespace). Fix the whole try block: import aiida-core's orm/engine from the installed package and import the Crystal workchains from their actual tui path (or migrate them into crystalmath) without shadowing the aiida namespace.

title: [guard-silent-simulated-stub-results] Stop silently returning {simulated:True} success when no runner is configured
description:
BaseAnalysisRunner._execute_step (python/crystalmath/high_level/runners.py:1177-1187) returns StepResult(success=True, outputs={'simulated': True}) with only a warning when self._runner is None. All four runner subclasses (StandardAnalysis, PhononAnalysis, ElasticAnalysis, TransportAnalysis) inherit this. _create_slurm_runner (379-411) returns None on any import error (including the deprecated-tui import failure), so a user passing cluster=beefcake2 can silently land in stub mode. Over IPC these fake-success results are indistinguishable from real ones to the Rust TUI. The AGENTS.md allow_stub_execution gate exists in the deprecated tui/src/core/workflow.py:1085-1091 but was never ported to the Python-core runners (verification confirmed the gate exists in the engine, so the broader 'gate entirely absent' claim was refuted — the real gap is the unguarded simulated path in high_level/runners). Fix: require metadata['allow_stub_execution']=True (per AGENTS.md) before returning a simulated result, otherwise raise a distinct error.

DEPENDS ON (slugs): migrate-tui-imports-into-core

title: [parsl-runner-configure-executor] Configure Parsl (SlurmProvider/HighThroughputExecutor) in ParslRunner
description:
ParslRunner.submit (python/crystalmath/quacc/parsl_runner.py:54-74) calls recipe_func(atoms, **kwargs) directly with no preceding parsl.load(); ParslClusterConfig (config.py:22-63) stores partition/nodes/walltime but has no to_parsl_config() and cluster_name is forwarded but ignored. Result: quacc recipes execute synchronously in-process (the server thread) rather than dispatching to SLURM — the 'future' returned is a plain dict, and get_status/get_result hedge for that. This is the live jobs.submit path via server/handlers/jobs.py. Add to_parsl_config() building a parsl.Config with HighThroughputExecutor+SlurmProvider, call parsl.load() in ParslRunner.__init__, and route cluster_name to the right executor label so non-local engines dispatch to SLURM.

title: [multistep-workflow-execution] Implement multi-step workflow execution: dependency chaining + wait-for-completion + inter-step data handoff
description:
Multi-step scientific workflows (relax->SCF->bands/DOS, GW->BSE, phonon, VASP->YAMBO) are non-functional in the production runner path. Three linked defects: (1) BaseAnalysisRunner._execute_workflow (python/crystalmath/high_level/runners.py:1118-1159) submits steps sequentially but submit_async (slurm_runner.py:395-450) returns immediately after submission without polling, so step N+1 fires while step N is still PENDING/RUNNING; (2) step.outputs_to_pass is declared at 6 sites (runners.py:1533/1551/1706/1724/2320/2334) but _execute_step (1161-1214) never reads it or transfers WAVECAR/CHGCAR/ndb.* between steps; (3) SLURM dependency chaining (--dependency=afterok) is not implemented (submit_composite_async at slurm_runner.py:1191-1222 stashes pending_steps in metadata that nothing ever reads). Implement either real SLURM afterok dependency chaining or a poll-until-complete loop that stages outputs_to_pass files into the next step's work_dir before submitting it. (Note: the atomate2 submit_composite/MultiCodeFlowBuilder stubs are a separate Phase-3 path, not the blocker — verification downgraded those to P2/P3.)

description:
SLURMWorkflowRunner stores submitted jobs only in self._jobs (python/crystalmath/integrations/slurm_runner.py:267) with no persistence. After an IPC-server restart, get_status (1246), get_result (1315) and cancel (1525) return failed/False for every previously-submitted workflow_id — live cluster jobs are silently orphaned from the tracking layer (the SLURM jobs keep running, but the user can no longer poll/cancel them). title: [ci-run-rust-tests-and-ipc-build] Run cargo test and the IPC (--no-default-features) build automatically in CI
description:
ci-rust.yml gates rust-tests behind workflow_dispatch only, so cargo test (including 40+ unit tests in src/app_tests.rs and the IPC integration tests) never runs on PR/push. The rust-quality job runs clippy with default (pyo3-bridge) features only — the IPC transport (src/ipc/*, src/bridge_ipc.rs, the cfg(not(pyo3-bridge)) main.rs branch) is never built or linted in automated CI, so IPC regressions go undetected. The default-feature clippy also has an implicit python3-dev header dependency with no setup step. Add automatic steps: `cargo test --no-default-features` and `cargo build --no-default-features` + `cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings` on src/** PRs. Integration tests already honor CRYSTALMATH_SKIP_IPC_TESTS for environments without a live server.

title: [validate-recipe-import-allowlist] Allowlist recipe string before __import__ in jobs.submit IPC handler
description:
python/crystalmath/server/handlers/jobs.py:110 takes user-supplied `recipe` from the IPC request and checks only truthiness, then passes it unchanged to runner.submit(recipe_fullname=recipe) which calls __import__(module_path, fromlist=[func_name]) in python/crystalmath/quacc/runner.py:134. Any IPC client (any local process running as the same user — socket is chmod 0600, which scopes but does not eliminate the risk) can cause arbitrary module import / import-time side-effect execution. Verification corrected severity from P0 to P1 because the 0600 socket blocks remote-unauthenticated RCE. Fix: validate recipe against discover_vasp_recipes() output or assert recipe.startswith('quacc.recipes.') in handle_jobs_submit, and add the same defense-in-depth check in _import_recipe (runner.py:116).

design:
## Deepening design — C1-rust-tui-handlers
**Risk:** medium · **Depends on IPC cutover:** False

### Current shape (shallowness / friction)
The cluster-manager domain is physically split across three files with no single owning module, verified by grep:

1. STATE — src/ui/cluster_manager.rs:87-336. `ClusterManagerState` struct (fields: active, closing, loading, mode, clusters, selected_index, form fields, editing_cluster_id, request_id, error, connection_result) plus its impl methods (open/close/reset_form/start_add/start_edit/start_delete/cancel/select_next/select_prev/selected_cluster/build_config/set_status) and the rendering free fns (render at :338, render_list_view, render_form_view, render_delete_confirm — all taking `&App`, not `&ClusterManagerState`, so they couple to the whole App).

2. INPUT DISPATCH — src/main.rs:1154-1282. `fn handle_cluster_manager_modal_input(app: &mut App, key)` (129 lines), a free function (not a method, not on any trait) that matches on `app.cluster_manager.mode` and pokes `app.cluster_manager.*` plus calls App orchestration methods. It is one of 17 `handle_*` free fns in main.rs (15 are per-modal), all dispatched by a 40+ line if/else-if ladder at src/main.rs:446-506 keyed on `app.is_*_modal_active()` + `!app.<state>.closing`.

3. RESPONSE HANDLING — src/app.rs. Six BridgeResponse arms (Clusters :1214, Cluster :1234, ClusterCreated :1241, ClusterUpdated :1260, ClusterDeleted :1276, ClusterConnectionTested :1298) inside `poll_bridge_responses` (src/app.rs:737, ~25 top-level BridgeResponse arms total in the 737-2034 range). The same six variants are ALSO enumerated twice more in that fn: request_id extraction at :775-780 and pending-match filtering at :806-811. Plus orchestration methods on App: open/close_cluster_manager_modal :3971/:3978, request_fetch_clusters :3984, create/update_cluster_from_form :3997/:4018, delete_selected_cluster :4052, test_selected_cluster_connection :4081.

So one logical domain = one struct + one free fn + six match arms (x3 enumerations) + seven App methods, spread over 3 files. The transport below is already deep: `App.bridge: Box<dyn BridgeService>` (src/app.rs:114), trait at src/bridge.rs:118 with typed default methods funneling through `request_rpc` (PyO3 in bridge.rs, IPC in bridge_ipc.rs) — DO NOT TOUCH per instructions.

### Deepened interface
A `Handler` trait that makes the per-domain interface the test surface. Caller (App as thin coordinator) must know only:

trait Handler {
  fn is_active(&self) -> bool;
  fn handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent, bridge: &dyn BridgeService, ctx: &mut HandlerCtx) -> bool; // owns input->Action; returns "consumed"
  fn handle_response(&mut self, resp: &BridgeResponse, ctx: &mut HandlerCtx) -> bool; // returns "I claimed this response"
  fn render(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect); // renders from OWN state, not &App
}

HandlerCtx carries the cross-cutting bits a handler legitimately needs without reaching into all of App: next_request_id(), set_error(), mark_dirty(), and a follow-up queue (so e.g. ClusterCreated can enqueue a re-fetch instead of calling self.request_fetch_clusters()). The handler owns ClusterManagerState internally; the interface exposes none of those fields.

This is DEEP: behind ~4 methods sits all of build_config validation, the List/Add/Edit/ConfirmDelete mode machine, the six-variant response routing, request_id correlation, and rendering. Leverage: App stops knowing cluster internals entirely — today App has 7 cluster methods + 6+6+6 inline match arms; after, App holds `Box<dyn Handler>` and forwards. The interface IS the test surface: handle_key/handle_response become unit-testable in isolation against a MockBridgeService (src/bridge.rs:160-207 already records requests as strings) — today the 129-line handle_cluster_manager_modal_input free fn in main.rs has ZERO unit tests because free fns over &mut App are not reachable without a full App.

Deletion test: delete the cluster Handler and complexity does NOT vanish — it REAPPEARS as the input ladder arm in main.rs, the 6 response arms + 2 enumerations in app.rs, and the orphaned ClusterManagerState. It earns its keep by collapsing 3 call sites into 1.

### Seam & adapters
The Handler trait is a REAL seam (not hypothetical) the moment a second domain implements it — and there are 15 modal candidates already (handle_*_modal_input fns in main.rs: materials :659, new_job :968, cluster_manager :1154, slurm_queue :1345, vasp_input :1410, recipe_browser :1479, workflow_modal :1520, workflow_config :1569, template_browser :1774, batch_submission :1815, output_viewer :1902, help :1979), each mirrored by a render dispatch in src/ui/mod.rs (cluster at :168) and response arms in app.rs. That is N>=2 adapters guaranteed, so the seam is justified, not speculative.

Crucial constraint already satisfied: the Handler seam sits ABOVE the BridgeService seam (src/bridge.rs:118), which is already deep and DI'd via `Box<dyn BridgeService>`. handle_key/handle_response take `&dyn BridgeService` / `&BridgeResponse` — both transport-agnostic types that exist identically under PyO3 and IPC. So Handler does NOT touch bridge.rs/bridge_ipc.rs/lsp.rs and is orthogonal to the PyO3->IPC cutover.

Adapters: each existing modal becomes a Handler impl. Pilot adapter = ClusterHandler wrapping ClusterManagerState. The App-side adapter is a registry/dispatch: `handlers: Vec<Box<dyn Handler>>` replacing the if/else ladder (main.rs:446-506) and the per-modal response arms (app.rs poll_bridge_responses). Locality win: a cluster bug or a new cluster feature lives in ONE file (ClusterHandler) instead of being hunted across main.rs + app.rs + ui/cluster_manager.rs.

### Migration steps
Strangler-fig, pilot cluster-manager only, behaviour-preserving:

1. Add src/handlers/mod.rs defining `trait Handler` + `HandlerCtx` (next_request_id/set_error/mark_dirty/follow-up queue). No behaviour change.
2. Add src/handlers/cluster.rs: `ClusterHandler { state: ClusterManagerState }`. MOVE (don't rewrite) the body of handle_cluster_manager_modal_input (main.rs:1154-1282) into handle_key; MOVE the 6 response-arm bodies (app.rs:1214-1317) into handle_response (matching on the 6 Cluster* variants, returning true when claimed); MOVE the 7 orchestration methods (app.rs:3971-4113) onto the handler, swapping `self.bridge.x` for `bridge.x` and `self.request_fetch_clusters()` for ctx.enqueue_followup(FetchClusters). render delegates to ui::cluster_manager but refactored to take &ClusterManagerState.
3. In App: replace the `cluster_manager: ClusterManagerState` field with `cluster: ClusterHandler` (or keep field, wrap). In poll_bridge_responses, before the big match, give handlers first refusal: `for h in &mut handlers { if h.handle_response(&response, &mut ctx) { continue; } }` and delete the 6 cluster arms + their entries in the :775-780 and :806-811 enumerations. In main.rs input ladder, replace the cluster else-if with handler dispatch.
4. Run cargo test (cluster behaviour identical; existing 57 app.rs tests must pass unchanged since public App API can keep thin shims).
5. Once green, repeat per modal in later PRs. This is a SEPARATE beads epic; sequence AFTER the keystone PyO3->IPC cutover only if it would otherwise churn app.rs concurrently — technically independent, but coordinate to avoid merge conflicts in app.rs/main.rs.

### Tests (survive / add)
SURVIVE: The 57 #[test] in src/app.rs and 55 in src/app_tests.rs construct App with `Box::new(MockBridgeService::new())` (app.rs:5676, app_tests.rs:343). If App keeps thin public shims (open_cluster_manager_modal etc. delegating to the handler), these survive unchanged. MockBridgeService (src/bridge.rs:160-207) already records request strings like "FetchClusters(request_id=..)" / "CreateCluster(..)" — reusable as-is for handler tests.

ADD (the real payoff — these are NEW, impossible today): direct unit tests on the Handler interface, the new test surface:
- ClusterHandler::handle_key in Add mode with Enter calls bridge.request_create_cluster (assert via MockBridgeService recorded requests) — today handle_cluster_manager_modal_input (main.rs:1154) is a free fn over &mut App with NO unit tests at all (grep for cluster tests in app_tests.rs found only the default-field construction at :343, zero input tests).
- ClusterHandler::handle_response(ClusterCreated{Ok}) sets status + enqueues a FetchClusters follow-up; (Err) sets error status — replacing untested inline arms.
- build_config validation already lives on the state and is unit-testable; pull it under handler tests.
Net: the cluster input+response logic goes from 0% unit-tested to directly testable behind the interface.

### Obvious-fix verdict
safe=False scope=rust
scope=rust by definition: every cited symbol is in src/*.rs, zero Python touched (grep confirmed cluster logic in python/ is unrelated backend code in models.py/api.py, reached only through the already-deep BridgeService RPC seam). The change is structural across two of the largest files in the repo and behaviour-sensitive (input routing + response correlation via request_id at app.rs:760-811), so it cannot be mechanically applied or verified by a targeted import. safe=false.
acceptance_criteria:
- A Handler trait (is_active/handle_key(&dyn BridgeService,&mut HandlerCtx)/handle_response(&BridgeResponse,&mut HandlerCtx)/render) + HandlerCtx exists; render reads own state, not &App.
- cluster-manager is ported to a ClusterHandler with unit tests against MockBridgeService (impossible today); its main.rs input free-fn and the 6 app.rs Cluster* response arms are deleted.
- App holds Box<dyn Handler> dispatch; the existing app.rs/app_tests.rs suites pass unchanged.
- Remaining modals tracked for follow-up.
notes: Pilot landed (commit on refactor/arch-deepening): Handler trait + ClusterManagerState adapter, behaviour-preserving, +7 isolation tests, full Rust suite green, 0 new clippy warnings. Remaining: 3y4.3 (decouple render from &App; generalize App to Vec<Box<dyn Handler>> dispatch and delete the main.rs input ladder + app.rs response match).

design:
## Deepening design — C6-one-config-resolver
**Risk:** medium · **Depends on IPC cutover:** True

### Current shape (shallowness / friction)
Path/config resolution is duplicated across languages, "kept in sync by hand" via doc comments. Two distinct concerns are conflated under one candidate:

1. SOCKET PATH (3-tier: XDG_RUNTIME_DIR -> macOS Caches -> /tmp/{uid}). Two real implementations:
   - Python: python/crystalmath/server/__init__.py:58 get_default_socket_path() -> Path. Fallback line 79: `/tmp/crystalmath-{uid}.sock`.
   - Rust: src/ipc/client.rs:174 default_socket_path() -> PathBuf. Fallback line 188: `/tmp/crystalmath-{uid}.sock`.
   These ALREADY AGREE. The "drift" the candidate flags (/tmp/crystalmath.sock vs uid-scoped) is NOT present in any live resolver — git blame shows Python line 79 was uid-scoped from its first commit (00248df, feat 01-01, 2026-02-02). The non-uid literal `/tmp/crystalmath.sock` survives ONLY in Rust doc-comment examples: src/ipc.rs:33, src/ipc/client.rs:355, src/ipc/client.rs:387 (illustrative `IpcClient::connect("...")` snippets, not the resolver). One genuine behavioural nuance remains: Python's macOS branch gates on `if cache_dir.exists()` (line 74) while Rust's `dirs::cache_dir()` does not check existence — a latent divergence if ~/Library/Caches were ever absent, but not the drift claimed.

2. DATABASE PATH (6-tier .crystal_tui.db / jobs.db resolution). Two real implementations:
   - Python: python/crystalmath/backends/__init__.py:60 find_database_path() -> str | None.
   - Rust: src/bridge.rs:458 find_database_path() -> Option<String>.
   Mirrored by hand; the Python docstring (lines 63-73) explicitly says "Mirrors the Rust resolver".

The candidate's "Bash also reimplements this" claim is OVERSTATED: cli/lib/cry-config.sh resolves a DIFFERENT, non-overlapping set of paths (CRY23_ROOT, CRY_BIN_DIR, scratch, tutorials) — grep for `.sock` / `crystal_tui.db` / `database` in cli/ returns NOTHING. Bash is not a third copy of the socket/DB resolver; it is a separate config module. No `--export-bash` or handshake mechanism exists anywhere (grep export-bash/export_bash/handshake = empty).

Callers verified: socket resolver has 1 Python caller (server/__init__.py:130, plus argparse help string :501) and 3 Rust use-sites (src/main.rs:371, integration test tests/ipc_integration.rs:322, unit test src/ipc/client.rs:646). DB resolver: Python find_database_path called at cli.py:44; Rust find_database_path at bridge.rs:436 and ipc/client.rs:295.

### Deepened interface
A single deep Module in the Python core whose Interface is "give me the resolved runtime config" — one pydantic-settings object (per ADR-015) exposing resolved values as plain fields:
  CrystalmathSettings.socket_path: Path
  CrystalmathSettings.database_path: Path | None
  CrystalmathSettings.from_env() -> CrystalmathSettings  (single resolution entrypoint)

The Interface is tiny (a few typed fields + one constructor) but the Implementation is Deep: it absorbs the entire 3-tier socket cascade and 6-tier DB cascade, the env overrides (XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, CRYSTAL_TUI_DB), the macOS/Linux platform branching, and the directory-creation-on-resolve side effects. Every caller — Python server, Python CLI, Rust TUI, Bash CLI — must know only "ask the resolver for the resolved value," never the cascade rules. That is Leverage: the cascade lives behind one small interface, and Locality is restored — a path-resolution bug or rule change is fixed in exactly one place instead of reappearing across the Python+Rust+(future Bash) copies. The Deletion test passes: delete this module and the 3-tier+6-tier cascade complexity REAPPEARS in N callers (it already does, in 2 languages), so it earns its keep.

Non-Python consumers read RESOLVED values rather than re-deriving them:
  - Rust reads resolved socket/DB paths over the IPC handshake (a `system.resolveConfig` JSON-RPC call) once the IPC cutover lands, deleting src/ipc/client.rs:174 and src/bridge.rs:458 resolvers (leaving only thin connect-to-given-path code).
  - Bash, IF it ever needs the socket/DB paths, reads them via a `crystalmath config --export-bash` subcommand that prints `export CRYSTALMATH_SOCKET=...` lines, instead of growing a third copy.

### Seam & adapters
The Seam is the resolved-config boundary in the Python core: one resolver object, consumed by adapters that translate it to each runtime.

Adapter count today = TWO real consumers of duplicated resolution logic, so this is a REAL seam, not hypothetical:
  Adapter 1 (Python): server/__init__.py and cli.py call the resolver directly (in-process) and use the typed fields.
  Adapter 2 (Rust): currently a hand-mirrored COPY (the anti-adapter). The redesign turns it into a true thin adapter that fetches resolved values over IPC (`system.resolveConfig` handshake) and never re-derives the cascade.
  Adapter 3 (Bash, latent/hypothetical): `--export-bash` adapter; only one consumer would exist, so on its own it would be merely a hypothetical seam — but combined with the two real ones it is justified as part of the same boundary.

"The interface is the test surface": today the cascade is tested in two unrelated places — python/tests/test_db_path_resolution.py (5 cases, asserting parity with src/bridge.rs by mirroring its order) and the Rust unit/integration tests src/ipc/client.rs:644 test_default_socket_path_format + tests/ipc_integration.rs:321. After deepening, the cascade is tested ONCE against the Python resolver interface; the Rust side only needs a contract test that the handshake returns what the resolver produced.

### Migration steps
1. Create crystalmath/config.py with a pydantic-settings BaseSettings model (ADR-015) exposing socket_path and database_path. Lift the EXACT cascades from server/__init__.py:58-79 and backends/__init__.py:60-127 into its resolvers; keep behaviour byte-identical (including macOS cache_dir.exists() gating and CRYSTAL_TUI_DB mkdir side effects).
2. Repoint Python callers: server/__init__.py:130 (`socket_path or get_default_socket_path()`) and cli.py:44 (`find_database_path()`) to the new resolver. Keep get_default_socket_path/find_database_path as thin shims initially so existing tests (test_db_path_resolution.py) keep passing, then migrate the tests to target the resolver interface.
3. Add a `system.resolveConfig` JSON-RPC method to the server returning resolved socket_path + database_path.
4. (GATED ON IPC CUTOVER) After epic ipc-cutover-delete-pyo3 lands, delete src/ipc/client.rs:174 default_socket_path() and src/bridge.rs:458 find_database_path(); Rust reads resolved values from the handshake. This step touches Rust and depends on the cutover — keep it out of any pre-cutover PR.
5. (OPTIONAL) Add `crystalmath config --export-bash`; only wire cli/lib/cry-config.sh to it if a real second Bash consumer of socket/DB paths appears (today there is none).
6. Fix the macOS cache_dir.exists() vs Rust dirs::cache_dir() nuance in the single resolver so the divergence cannot recur.

### Tests (survive / add)
SURVIVE: python/tests/test_db_path_resolution.py (5 cases at lines 18,24,32,41,50) must stay green — they assert the 6-tier DB cascade outcomes and are the de-facto contract; migrate them to call the new resolver but keep the same assertions. Rust src/ipc/client.rs:644 test_default_socket_path_format and tests/ipc_integration.rs:321 stay green until step 4 deletes the Rust resolver, then are replaced by a handshake contract test.
ADD: (a) a pytest for get_default_socket_path / resolver.socket_path — NONE EXISTS today (grep confirms no Python test asserts the socket path; the apparent matches in tests are beads issue IDs like crystalmath-6l8); add cases for XDG_RUNTIME_DIR set, macOS cache present/absent, and the /tmp/{uid} fallback. (b) a parity/contract test that the Rust handshake value equals the Python resolver value (replacing the hand-mirroring documented in test_db_path_resolution.py:1-3).

### Obvious-fix verdict
safe=False scope=none
Mark obvious only if a bounded, Python-only, cutover-independent change applies NOW and improves things. Here the alleged drift is already resolved, so the only candidate edits are either no-ops (Python) or Rust doc-comment cosmetics (forbidden: Rust, not cutover-independent in spirit, fragile build). The real value (one deep pydantic-settings resolver, ADR-015) is a cross-language redesign gated on the IPC cutover (Cargo.toml:15 default=["pyo3-bridge"] still live) — design-only.

title: Replace bespoke ADAPTIVE recovery with custodian-style per-code error handlers
description: Today recovery is a bespoke design only: protocols.py ErrorRecoveryStrategy.ADAPTIVE (l.121) + an unimplemented ErrorHandler Protocol, plus a fork-vendored VASPErrorHandler (_vendor/runners/vasp_errors.py) used as a fallback in server/handlers/jobs.py:596 with an inline regex _analyze_vasp_errors_fallback. custodian is not a dependency. Adopt custodian's wrap-detect-patch-restart loop beneath the jobflow engine: the maintained VaspErrorHandler/QE catalogue plus thin CRYSTAL23/YAMBO ErrorHandler subclasses CrystalMath owns; graph-level recovery via jobflow Response. Delete the bespoke ADAPTIVE machinery, substring-grep _is_retryable_error, and the vendored vasp_errors patterns. Realizes ADR-018.
design:
ADRs realized: ADR-018
Depends on epics: jobflow-ir-and-handoff

title: Adopt jobflow-remote as the one HPC execution backend; demote bespoke SLURM/SSH, AiiDA opt-in
description: Multiple co-equal runner stacks (the exact N-way facade ADR-007 names): integrations/slurm_runner.py (~1.5k LOC bespoke sbatch-over-SSH), _vendor/runners/slurm_runner.py (~1.8k LOC), high_level/runners.py + clusters.py, quacc parsl/covalent/mock runners, and workflows/aiida_launcher.py. jobflow-remote/PSI-J/QToolKit are NOT dependencies. Adopt one ExecutionBackend protocol with jobflow-remote's outbound-SSH polling daemon as default and AiiDA as the single opt-in heavyweight; delete the ~3.3k LOC of hand-rolled SLURM/SSH stacks. Note bd-4m6 (configure Parsl as the SLURM dispatch seam) conflicts with ADR-012, which reserves Parsl/Dask as in-allocation executors only — reframe it as in-allocation under jobflow-remote, not the SSH boundary. Fix the AiiDA launcher import bug (bd-hnq) since AiiDA remains the opt-in backend. Realizes ADR-012.

title: Extract crystalmath/decks/ deep input-deck generator from SLURMWorkflowRunner
description: Deepening from a /improve-codebase-architecture grilling (design in CONTEXT.md). The SLURM runner (~2000 lines) fuses DFT input-deck generation with SSH transport/scripting/persistence, and reimplements VASP generation inline (duplicating crystalmath/vasp/generator.py). Extract a deep seam: crystalmath/decks/ with a CodeDeckGenerator interface (generate(structure, workflow_type, parameters) -> InputDeck), per-code adapters (Vasp absorbs vasp/generator.py, Crystal wraps the vendored crystal_d12, Qe, Yambo) looked up via a registry, an InputDeck content value object (file contents as strings + POTCAR symbols + metadata, no I/O), and a separate stage(deck, work_dir) that writes files and assembles POTCAR from VASP_PP_PATH (fail-fast — the d4l behavior moves here). SLURMWorkflowRunner shrinks to generate->stage->submit/poll/retrieve. Enables a LocalWorkflowRunner (generate->stage->subprocess) as a 2nd WorkflowRunner adapter. Relates to drm, d4l, and candidate 4.
acceptance_criteria: crystalmath/decks/ exists with CodeDeckGenerator + registry + InputDeck + stage(); VaspDeckGenerator/CrystalDeckGenerator/QeDeckGenerator/YamboDeckGenerator produce correct InputDeck content asserted THROUGH the generator interface (no work_dir, no cluster); stage() owns file writing + POTCAR fail-fast with its own tests; vasp/generator.py is absorbed (one VASP generator); SLURMWorkflowRunner delegates to decks and shrinks; suite green.
close_reason: Extracted crystalmath/decks: InputDeck + CodeDeckGenerator seam + registry + 4 per-code adapters (Vasp/Crystal/Qe/Yambo) + stage() with POTCAR fail-fast. SLURMWorkflowRunner delegates (generate->stage), inline generators deleted (-452 lines, 2004->1552); duplicated VASP generation removed (adapter wraps vasp/generator.py). Done TDD in 5 vertical slices; deck-content tests now assert through the interface. 518 passed default; no regressions.

title: [pre-existing] pymatgen-version test failures in test_pymatgen_bridge + test_vasp_generator
description: With pymatgen installed, 5 tests fail on clean HEAD (independent of crystalmath-z46/d4l/drm/b0d): test_pymatgen_bridge.py::test_real_symmetry_analysis, ::test_full_workflow_cif_to_validation; test_vasp_generator.py::test_generate_complete_inputs (+2). title: [implement-highlevel-api-or-mark-stub] Implement (or clearly defer) HighThroughput/WorkflowBuilder execution methods
description:
The high-level API is part-stub. run_standard_analysis and from_mp work in mock/local mode (verification refuted the 'entirely stub' claim), but Workflow.run/run_async/submit/get_status/get_result/cancel all raise NotImplementedError (builder.py:215-328), from_poscar/from_structure/from_aiida raise NotImplementedError (api.py:364/401/436), and run_standard_analysis uses the complete_workflow() testing hook to auto-complete in mock mode with no real cluster polling (api.py:265-267); the cluster= param (api.py:156) is silently ignored. from_structure/from_poscar are one-line delegations to run_standard_analysis (the _load_structure helper at api.py:444-488 already handles paths and Structures). Either implement Workflow.run by delegating to the runner infrastructure and wire real polling, or remove the unimplemented surface from __all__ and document it as deferred so callers do not hit confusing NotImplementedErrors.

AREA: High-level API
EFFORT: XL | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/high_level/api.py, python/crystalmath/high_level/builder.py, python/crystalmath/high_level/runners.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): multistep-workflow-execution, parsl-runner-configure-executor
acceptance_criteria: Either Workflow.run / from_poscar / from_structure execute a real (or properly mocked) workflow with cluster routing honored, or the unimplemented methods are removed from the public API and documented as deferred; no advertised public method raises a bare NotImplementedError at runtime.

description:
ci-cli.yml:30 runs `bats cli/tests/integration/*.bats` with continue-on-error: true, so broken end-to-end CLI tests do not block merges even though the CLI is marked Production in AGENTS.md. The integration tests use mock binaries (cli/tests/mocks/crystalOMP), so there is no inherent CI limitation. Remove continue-on-error, or scope it only to tests that genuinely need real CRYSTAL23 binaries (guarded and skipped in CI).

DEPENDS ON (slugs): none

notes: INVESTIGATED, NOT hard-gated yet. Running 'bats cli/tests/integration/*.bats' locally (macOS) shows MANY failures (tests 2,4,16-22,44,52,... — explain-mode, no-gum, output-retrieval). These are very likely macOS-specific (BSD vs GNU coreutils, bash 3.2, missing gum/timeout), but I cannot verify the Linux CI result from here. NEXT: run the integration suite on ubuntu-latest CI (or a Linux container), triage real failures, skip tests needing real CRYSTAL23 binaries, THEN remove continue-on-error. ci-cli.yml left unchanged.

title: [fix-phonon-workflow-rpc-handler] Fix create_phonon_workflow_json runtime bugs (wrong names/methods crash the handler)
description:
create_phonon_workflow_json (python/crystalmath/api.py) crashes before returning due to multiple mismatches against phonon.py: imports DFTCode but the class is PhononDFTCode (api.py:2527 vs phonon.py:26); constructs PhononMethod('finite_displacement') which is not a valid member (phonon.py:18-23); passes symmetry= but the field is use_symmetry= (phonon.py:59); calls workflow.generate_displacements(structure) which does not exist (only generate_displacements_phonopy); references result.num_displacements vs n_displacements; and returns key 'displacements' not 'inputs' so the Rust job-submission loop (app.rs:1412) finds no jobs. (The convergence-study path is correctly wired end-to-end — that part of the broader 'no execution glue' claim was refuted.) Fix the import/method/field names and return an 'inputs' array consistent with the Rust submit loop.

EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False

DEPENDS ON (slugs): none

title: [python-db-path-resolution-cli] Mirror Rust find_database_path in Python so the CLI/server open the same DB
description:
DB path resolution lives only in Rust (src/bridge.rs:454-563: CRYSTAL_TUI_DB env, project-root, CWD, XDG, platform data dir). The IPC server is covered because the Rust client injects CRYSTAL_TUI_DB into the spawned server (src/ipc/client.rs:230-232). But the standalone Python CLI (python/crystalmath/cli.py:37-39) has no env-var resolution and defaults to None, which falls through to DemoBackend (backends/__init__.py:180-193) — a user running `crystal list` without CRYSTAL_TUI_DB silently operates against demo data. Verification downgraded P1->P2 (primary Rust+IPC path is correct; CLI is secondary; wrong data not crash). Implement a Python find_database_path mirroring the Rust chain and call it as the fallback in CrystalController.__init__ and the CLI.

AREA: Config / packaging
EFFORT: M | BLOCKER: False

DEPENDS ON (slugs): none

close_reason: find_database_path() added to backends mirroring the Rust chain; CLI _get_controller uses it so 'crystal list' opens the real DB instead of DemoBackend. Controller default left unchanged to protect demo-mode tests. 5 hermetic tests; 496 passed.

title: [batch-submission-response-tracking] Track per-job results from batch submission instead of discarding request IDs
description:
submit_batch (src/app.rs:5041-5071) generates a request_id per job, fires request_submit_job, discards the result with `let _ =`, never stores the id, then immediately closes the modal and shows a generic status regardless of actual success/failure. The user cannot tell which batch jobs succeeded. Store each request_id in a batch-tracking map, update the JobSubmitted handler to mark per-job status in batch_submission.jobs[i], and close the modal only once all responses arrive or a timeout elapses.

AREA: Rust TUI
EFFORT: M | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: src/app.rs
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: Batch submission shows per-job submitted/failed status driven by actual JobSubmitted responses; the modal stays open until all responses arrive or it times out; covered by an app test.

title: [wire-template-browser-render] Make Template Browser Enter render the template into the editor
description:
Template Browser Enter (src/main.rs:1841-1848) has a TODO and just closes the modal without sending an RPC. The BridgeResponse::TemplateRendered handler (src/app.rs:1334-1341) only debug-logs the byte count and drops the content — request_render_template is wired but only invoked from test mocks. Verification downgraded P1->P2 (secondary feature; core D12/Materials path unaffected). Fix: on Enter call app.request_render_template(name, default_params); in the TemplateRendered Ok arm call self.open_file(filename, rendered) and switch to the Editor tab, mirroring the D12Generated handling at app.rs:1095-1098.

title: [unify-jsonrpc-dispatch-registries] Unify the two JSON-RPC dispatch registries (HANDLER_REGISTRY vs CrystalController._rpc_registry)
description:
There are two parallel dispatch namespaces. The Rust bridge sends legacy names (fetch_jobs, fetch_clusters, submit_job) that fall through to CrystalController.dispatch(), while server/handlers/*.py registers a dotted namespace (jobs.list, jobs.submit, clusters.list) checked first in server/__init__.py:260. app.rs also sends some dotted names (jobs.submit/jobs.status) via the untyped RpcResult path. The result is duplicate/overlapping methods (jobs.list, clusters.list, recipes.list registered in both, with different param handling and an unguarded JobStatus(status_str) ValueError in the handler), and orphaned handler modules. ADR-006 already flags this as a follow-up. Consolidate on one method-naming scheme end to end (Rust <-> server) and remove the duplicates; add try/except around JobStatus parsing.

AREA: crystalmath-server / dispatch
EFFORT: M | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/server/__init__.py, python/crystalmath/server/handlers/jobs.py, python/crystalmath/server/handlers/clusters.py, python/crystalmath/api.py, src/bridge.rs
DEPENDS ON (slugs): migrate-tui-imports-into-core
acceptance_criteria: Each RPC method is registered in exactly one place; Rust and Python agree on method names; invalid status strings return a structured error not an uncaught ValueError; an integration test exercises jobs.submit/status/cancel end to end.

title: [controller-init-error-surfacing] Surface controller/DB init failures instead of masking them as Method-not-found
description:
CrystalController init failures are swallowed (server/__init__.py:157-160 returns None on any exception). When controller is None and a method is not in HANDLER_REGISTRY, dispatch returns JSONRPC_METHOD_NOT_FOUND (line 277-280), which the Rust bridge maps to a generic 'Method not found' — the real init error (e.g. missing DB or tui import) never reaches the user. Separately, _init_sqlite's failure path sets _db=None silently, making all cluster/SLURM/job methods return NO_DATABASE. Add an eager startup health check that logs the real controller/DB init failure at WARN/ERROR, and return JSONRPC_INTERNAL_ERROR ('server not ready: <reason>') rather than METHOD_NOT_FOUND when the controller is None. Also replace asyncio.get_event_loop() at server/__init__.py:268 with get_running_loop() (deprecated in 3.10+).

AREA: crystalmath-server / dispatch
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/server/__init__.py, python/crystalmath/api.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): migrate-tui-imports-into-core
acceptance_criteria: A failed controller/DB init produces a clear logged reason and an INTERNAL_ERROR (with cause) to the client rather than a misleading METHOD_NOT_FOUND; get_running_loop is used; covered by a dispatch test.

title: [store-spawned-server-child-handle] Retain spawned crystalmath-server Child handle for clean shutdown
description:
src/ipc/client.rs:235 binds the spawned server to `_child` which drops at end of ensure_server_running(), so the Rust side permanently loses the ability to SIGTERM/kill/reap the server on TUI exit. IpcBridgeHandle::drop only signals the Rust worker, never the Python child, so the server lingers up to 300s (its own inactivity timeout at server/__init__.py:391-409) after the TUI quits. Verification downgraded P1->P2 (inactivity timeout bounds the leak; not a crash risk). Fix: store the Child in IpcBridgeHandle (or a OnceLock<Mutex<Child>>) and call child.kill()+child.wait() from IpcBridgeHandle::drop. Replace the socket-file-existence poll in ensure_server_running (lines 239-251) with an actual UnixStream::connect retry loop to close the accept-readiness race.

AREA: IPC keystone
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: src/ipc/client.rs, src/bridge_ipc.rs, python/crystalmath/server/__init__.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: On TUI exit the spawned server is terminated promptly (not after 300s); ensure_server_running succeeds via real connect attempts rather than file-existence + fixed sleep; an integration test covers spawn+shutdown.
close_reason: Spawned servers retained in SPAWNED_SERVERS registry + shutdown_spawned_servers() called from IpcBridgeHandle::drop (kill+reap). Readiness check now uses real connect-retry instead of socket-file poll. Both clippy configs + fmt clean; IPC tests pass.

title: [validate-sql-column-distinct] Add column-name validation to jobflow_store.distinct() (SQL injection)
description:
python/crystalmath/integrations/jobflow_store.py:566 interpolates the caller-supplied `field` directly into `SELECT DISTINCT {field} FROM ...` with no validation, and the criteria keys at 572-573 also skip the `_VALID_COLUMN` regex that find() applies (lines 428-434/451-452). Verification confirmed but downgraded to P2: SQLite is a local file with no network attack surface and the only current caller passes a literal. Still violates AGENTS.md security rules and must be fixed before production. Fix: reuse the _VALID_COLUMN regex (promote it to module level) and validate both `field` and criteria keys, raising ValueError on invalid names. (_collection_name is already validated in connect() and is safe.)

AREA: Security
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/integrations/jobflow_store.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: distinct() raises ValueError on a non-identifier field or criteria key; a unit test covers an injection attempt; _VALID_COLUMN is applied consistently across find()/distinct()/remove_docs().
close_reason: distinct() now validates field + criteria keys via shared _validate_column/_VALID_COLUMN; query() consolidated onto the same helper. 6 new injection tests; 486 passed. (remove_docs is still a NotImplementedError stub; must validate when implemented.)

title: Delegate results.py plotters/LaTeX to pymatgen plotters + pandas.to_latex

title: Harden CLI bats unit tests for bats >= 1.13 (local macOS)
description: The cli/tests/unit bats suite passes in CI (ubuntu-latest, apt bats ~1.2.x, ci-cli.yml hard gate) but ~20 unit tests fail under bats 1.13.0 on macOS. Causes observed: (1) 'WORK_DIR: unbound variable' in cry-scratch/cry-stage tests — under bats 1.13 + set -u, an assertion references WORK_DIR before scratch_create exports it (or scratch_create's mktemp path differs on macOS); (2) gum-backed cry-ui tests exit 127 ('ui_spin'/'ui_card' command-not-found inside run) — bats 1.13 BW01 strictness + the gum mock on PATH; (3) parallel_setup nprocs validation. Not a codebase regression (CI green); a forward-looking robustness fix so the suite passes on current bats. Fix: guard WORK_DIR refs / ensure exports under set -u, fix the gum mock PATH for ui_spin, and address BW01 'run exited 127' advisories.
acceptance_criteria:
- cli/tests/unit/*.bats pass on bats >= 1.13 on macOS and Linux.

title: [flaky] src/lsp.rs parallel tests race on CRYSTAL_NODE_PATH env var
description: Several src/lsp.rs tests (test_lsp_client_start_nonexistent_node_binary + siblings ~lsp.rs:1484/1733/1802) set/remove the process-global env var CRYSTAL_NODE_PATH concurrently. Under parallel scheduling one test's set_var('...nonexistent...') can be overwritten/removed by a sibling, so LspClient::start spawns successfully and the is_err() assertion fails (~1/run under load; passes serial with --test-threads=1). Pre-existing (src/lsp.rs unchanged in PR #10), surfaced by the Codex-fix workflow's stress runs. Fix: serialize the env-mutating lsp tests (a shared mutex or #[serial]) or pass the node path via a builder arg instead of process env.
close_reason: Fixed on refactor/arch-deepening: serialized env-var access across src/lsp.rs parallel tests with a module-level Mutex; lsp tests stable over repeated runs.

title: [harden-ssh-known-hosts-and-quoting] Harden SSH host-key handling and shell quoting in the SLURM runner
description:
Defense-in-depth items in python/crystalmath/integrations/slurm_runner.py: _get_known_hosts returns an empty tuple () via type:ignore when known_hosts is absent (572-577) — asyncssh behavior for () is undocumented and may skip verification; return None (system defaults) and warn instead. allow_insecure has no production guard (152) — refuse to set it True when CRYSTALMATH_ENV=production. scancel/squeue interpolate slurm_job_id without shlex.quote (1547/1285) — inconsistent with the quoted remote_dir; add shlex.quote for consistency even though the id is regex-constrained to digits. (SSH host-key verification defaults to secure / allow_insecure=False, which verification confirmed is the correct posture.)

AREA: Security / SLURM
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/integrations/slurm_runner.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: Missing known_hosts yields system-default verification with a warning, not silent skip; allow_insecure cannot be enabled in production env; scancel/squeue use shlex.quote; covered by tests.
close_reason: Production guard on allow_insecure (__post_init__ + CRYSTALMATH_ENV); shlex.quote on squeue/sacct/scancel job ids; documented secure fail-closed known_hosts=() (corrected the audit's insecure None suggestion). 2 new tests; 491 passed.

title: [resolve-ipc-dead-code] Wire or gate the 9 dead-code warnings on the IPC build
description:
`cargo build --no-default-features` emits 9 dead-code warnings. Confirmed genuine: VaspInputState::set_status (src/ui/vasp_input.rs:230) is bypassed by direct field assignment in app.rs (2010/2021/4223) — replacing those assignments with set_status() is the correct fix since it also clears the paired error field. RecipeBrowserState::new (src/ui/recipes.rs:44) and WorkflowState::new (src/ui/workflows.rs:44) are trivial Default wrappers used only in #[cfg(test)] blocks — gate with #[cfg(test)] or remove and use ::default() in tests. Needed before `-D warnings` can be applied to the IPC clippy CI step and before the default-feature flip.

AREA: Rust TUI
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: src/ui/vasp_input.rs, src/ui/recipes.rs, src/ui/workflows.rs, src/app.rs
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: `cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings` passes with no dead-code warnings; set_status is used on the production validation path; no behavior change.
close_reason: 9 IPC dead-code warnings resolved (set_status wired; test-only new() cfg-gated; pending API #[allow(dead_code)]'d w/ rationale; Default impls added). Went further: cargo clippy -D warnings now clean on BOTH feature configs (autofixes + 2 checked_div). fmt clean; tests green.
